lede: A look at May 17 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Julie Anne San Jose and Chris Henry (wide receiver), and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2014 Lao People's Liberation Army Air Force An-74 crash
events.desc: A military plane crash in northern Laos kills 17 people.

events.title: Pamir Airways Flight 112
events.desc: Pamir Airways Flight 112 crashes in Afghanistan's Shakardara District, killing 44.

events.title: The Troubles
events.desc: The Troubles: Thirty-three civilians are killed and 300 injured when the Ulster Volunteer Force (UVF) detonates four car bombs in Dublin and Monaghan, Ireland.

events.title: Supreme Court of the United States
events.desc: The United States Supreme Court hands down a unanimous decision in Brown v. Board of Education of Topeka, Kansas, outlawing racial segregation in public schools.

events.title: Protocol of Corfu
events.desc: The Protocol of Corfu is signed, recognising full autonomy to Northern Epirus under nominal Albanian sovereignty.

events.title: The Wonderful Wizard of Oz
events.desc: The children's novel The Wonderful Wizard of Oz, by L. Frank Baum, is first published in the United States. The first copy is given to the author's sister.

events.title: International Telecommunication Union
events.desc: The International Telegraph Union (later the International Telecommunication Union) is established in Paris.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: During the Vicksburg campaign, Union forces under John A. McClernand defeat a Confederate rearguard and capture around 1,700 men at the Battle of Big Black River Bridge.

events.title: Anne of Denmark
events.desc: Anne of Denmark is crowned Queen of Scotland.

events.title: Edward Stafford, 3rd Duke of Buckingham
events.desc: Edward Stafford, 3rd Duke of Buckingham, is executed for treason.

births.name: Wang Leehom
births.line: American-Taiwanese singer-songwriter, producer, actor, and director

births.name: Dennis Potter
births.line: English voice actor, director, producer, and screenwriter (died 1994)

births.name: Léon Gérin
births.line: Canadian lawyer, sociologist, and civil servant (died 1951)

deaths.text: Bud Anderson, American World War II flying ace
Mario Benedetti, Uruguayan journalist, author, and poet
Gündüz Kılıç, Turkish football player and coach
False Dmitry I, pretender to the Russian throne
Edward Stafford, 3rd Duke of Buckingham, Welsh politician, Lord High Constable of England

observances.text: Christian feast day: William Hobart Hare (Episcopal Church (USA))
